title: 2 killed in crash on Route 6 near Westport-Dartmouth town line
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two people died after a crash on Route 6 near the Westport-Dartmouth line on Friday evening.
Victims Tristan Bedient and Kate Aldrich were in a Toyota Camry, which sustained catastrophic damage in the collision.
Two occupants of the other vehicle, a Saab sedan, had non-life-threatening injuries; investigation is ongoing.

title: Bus driver strike ends in Merrimack Valley after nearly 2 weeks
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Merrimack Valley Transit workers secured a $4 hourly wage increase and preserved Saturday overtime in a new one-year deal.
The strike, involving over 130 drivers and mechanics, halted fare-free service from July 1, impacting multiple Massachusetts towns.
Normal bus service resumed Sunday after the union and transit officials reached an agreement following over six months of talks.

title: 5 takeaways as red-hot Red Sox rally past Mets, 3-2, for 9th straight victory
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox defeated the New York Mets 3-2 for their ninth consecutive win, tying their longest road sweep since 1977.
Brayan Bello delivered a strong relief performance, lowering his ERA to 6.06 with 4 1/3 innings, 2 hits, and 1 earned run.
Red Sox achieved a rare road sweep of nine games; however, the All-Star break could disrupt their current winning momentum.

title: Noah Kahan joins Fenway Music Hall of Fame after historic four-night run
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Noah Kahan was inducted into the Fenway Music Hall of Fame after concluding a four-night run at Fenway Park.
Kahan joins artists like Billy Joel and Paul McCartney as the Hall of Fame's fourth inductee.
Kahan's induction followed the release of his album 'The Great Divide' and a string of notable media appearances.

title: AG: Mass. to receive $730,000 from Cash App in alleged deception settlement
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts will receive $730,000 from Cash App parent Block Inc. under a $45 million multistate deceptive practices settlement.
Block Inc. allegedly misled consumers about Cash App's safety and failed to protect users from rising fraud, according to prosecutors.
The settlement requires Block to improve security, stop deceptive promotions, and enhance customer support for fraud resolution.

title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old Tyngsborough man allegedly took a police boat and posed as an officer on Lake Mascuppic.
Authorities responded to reports of unauthorized boat use, finding the man enforcing boating rules with emergency lights activated.
The man may face charges for impersonating a police officer; a court will decide if sufficient evidence exists.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 tall ships and military vessels from 20 countries arrived in Boston Harbor for the Sail Boston 250 event.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail as thousands of visitors gathered to celebrate Boston's maritime history.
Ships, including India's INS Sudarshini and Poland's Dar Mlodziezy, docked at various locations across Boston's waterfront.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox selected shortstop Jake Schaffner with the No. 20 overall pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner hit .356 with six home runs, 50 RBI, and 85 runs for UNC in 2026.
He had a .987 fielding percentage at shortstop and stole 26 bases during the season.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The 2026 Sail Boston event featured the USS Constitution leading a fleet of historic ships in the Grand Parade of Sail.
Spectators honored maritime history from Castle Island to Piers Park, with remarks from local figures highlighting East Boston's shipbuilding legacy.
International vessels, including the Bluenose Two and Schooner Adventure, showcased the event's focus on historical and educational experiences.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello was named the midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic for his poor performance.
Bello posted a 10.35 ERA in eight starts, leading to his demotion to Triple-A Worcester.
Despite poor starter stats, Bello excelled in relief with a 0.71 ERA over 25.1 innings for Boston.
